summary: Phase 5.A.1 (drift-detection sweep before Phase 5.A.2's renderer + input + ui implementation) complete. The sweep landed exactly the items pinned in docs/PHASES.md Phase 5 callout block: (1) Phase 4.A.2 nit N4 — replaced the (JSON as unknown as { parse(...) }).parse cast trick in src/atlas/loader.ts:122-125 with a clean JSON.parse(...) call preceded by `// eslint-disable-next-line determinism/no-float-arithmetic` plus a justification comment naming the data-ingestion boundary; (2) Phase 4.A.2 nit N5 — added a `dual-source constant equality (build-info ↔ vite plugin)` describe block in tests/build/atlas-binary-hash-plugin.test.ts asserting EMPTY_SHA256 and PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION match byte-for-byte between scripts/vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash.mjs and src/build-info.ts (the duplication is forced by the build pipeline since the .mjs plugin cannot transit @noble/hashes through the TypeScript pipeline at vite-config-load time); (3) Phase 4.A.2 nit N6 — relocated RecipeContext from src/atlas/recipes/floor.ts to a new src/atlas/recipes/types.ts module so the floor recipe is no longer an accidental shared-types module; floor.ts re-exports the type via `export type { RecipeContext }` for backward compatibility with src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts (which is in RULES_FILES and editing would bump rulesetVersion — left intentionally untouched); all 7 recipe files plus src/atlas/generate.ts and tests/atlas/registry.test.ts updated to import from the new module; (4) Phase 4.A.2 nit N7 — added tests/build/fflate-version-pin.test.ts that reads package.json directly and asserts devDependencies.fflate === '0.8.2' as the explicit complementary trip-wire to the implicit golden-hash check in atlas-encoder-cross-runtime; (5) docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: appended a new 'Phase 5 frozen contracts (renderer + input + ui)' section between Phase 4 contracts and Build-time constants (~100 new lines), pinning the renderer/input/ui read-only contracts on sim state, the lint-restricted imports for src/render/** (no core/streams.ts, no sim/combat.ts), the atlas-loader call site at run start using the addendum-N7 placeholder-refusal + ATLAS_MISSING + hash-mismatch error messages already implemented in src/atlas/loader.ts, the input descriptor's relationship to the Phase 1 Action schema (keypress → Action is configuration, no rulesetVersion bump), the HUD's read-only contract on RunState, the diagnostic-surface preservation rule (Phase 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B Playwright assertions must keep passing alongside the new playable-game UI), and three new layer-table entries (src/render/, src/input/, src/ui/ with imports-allowed and imports-forbidden lists each); also added two new rows to the lint rule inventory deferring the actual rule bodies to 5.A.2's `eslint.config.js` extension. Phase 3.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards (applyFloorEntry redundant param, dirOrdinalForStep export comment, ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES shared constant, tick unknown-action defensive type-check, e2e SIM_DIGEST duplication) intentionally deferred because none of those files (src/sim/{run,ai,combat,turn}.ts, tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts) are in Phase 5.A.2's scope — Phase 5.A.2 wires input → existing harness/tick → render without modifying tick itself. Code-reviewer verdict APPROVE with 0 blockers (artifacts/code-review-phase-5-A-1.md). Two of the three cosmetic findings addressed in the same approval pass: the trivial test-case migration (tests/atlas/registry.test.ts:12 imports from ./recipes/types now) and the lint-inventory doc gap (two new rows in ARCHITECTURE.md's lint rule inventory deferring the rule bodies to 5.A.2). The third (tsconfig.tsbuildinfo gitignore cleanup) is pre-existing and out of Phase 5.A.1 scope. All gates green: 824 tests passing across 54 test files; 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build 62.95 KB raw / 24.03 KB gzipped — byte-identical to HEAD of phase-4.B (the changes are imports-only refactors + a comment swap + docs additions + dual-source assertions, with no bundle delta).
suggested_commit_message: phase-5.A.1: drift sweep (Phase 4.A.2 N4-N7 nits + RecipeContext relocation + Phase 5 ARCHITECTURE.md frozen contracts)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm Phase 4.B foundation (HEAD of phase-4.B commits 484060e and 92ccfb8) had not drifted before Phase 5.A.1 implementation began, and confirm the 5.A.1 changes maintain the foundation's invariants. Phase 4 was end-to-end verified by the cross-OS matrix going green on all three OSes (run 25325869817).
verification_sprint.unit_tests: Pre-phase: 821 passed (54 test files at HEAD of phase-4.B was 53 + the no-transient-sentinel.test.ts additions; counted as 821 against 53 files). Post-phase: 824 passed across 54 test files. Net-new: +3 tests (2 in tests/build/atlas-binary-hash-plugin.test.ts asserting EMPTY_SHA256 and PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION dual-source equality + 1 in the new tests/build/fflate-version-pin.test.ts asserting the package.json pin).
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The new `// eslint-disable-next-line determinism/no-float-arithmetic` directive in src/atlas/loader.ts is correctly scoped (single-line disable; the bare JSON.parse call below it is the only line affected).
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors (TS strict + exactOptionalPropertyTypes + noUncheckedIndexedAccess). The RecipeContext relocation preserves the type identity via re-export from floor.ts; all 7 recipe files + generate.ts + tests/atlas/registry.test.ts compile against the new ./types module.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green; dist/assets/index-Bb6oVDw8.js = 62.95 KB raw / 24.03 KB gzipped. Byte-identical to HEAD of phase-4.B (the diff is imports-only refactors + comment swaps + docs additions + dual-source assertions; no bundle delta expected; well under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget; ~68% headroom).
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: No user-visible changes in 5.A.1 (the live diagnostic page is unchanged from the phase-4.B-approved state). The cross-OS matrix from phase-4.B continues to assert atlas determinism; the atlas binary itself is unchanged in 5.A.1 (no recipe / encoder / palette modifications).

phase_4_a_2_nit_N4_loader_eslint_disable.evidence: src/atlas/loader.ts:118-125 now reads as a clean `// eslint-disable-next-line determinism/no-float-arithmetic` directive followed by `const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(manifestText);`. The justification comment names the data-ingestion boundary and references the structural validation that follows in `loadAtlasFromBytes`. The previous (JSON as unknown as { parse(...) }).parse cast trick is removed. Lint passes (the disable is correctly single-line scoped).

phase_4_a_2_nit_N5_dual_source_equality.evidence: tests/build/atlas-binary-hash-plugin.test.ts adds a `dual-source constant equality (build-info ↔ vite plugin)` describe block. Two new tests use renamed-alias imports (BUILD_INFO_EMPTY_SHA256, BUILD_INFO_PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION) from src/build-info.ts and assert byte-equality against the .mjs plugin's exports of the same names. Both pass. The duplication itself is forced (the .mjs plugin is loaded at vite-config-load time and cannot transit @noble/hashes through the TypeScript pipeline); the assertions close the audit-readability gap so silent drift fails loudly.

phase_4_a_2_nit_N6_recipe_context_reloca.evidence: src/atlas/recipes/types.ts is the new canonical home of the RecipeContext type. src/atlas/recipes/floor.ts imports from ./types and re-exports via `export type { RecipeContext }` for backward compatibility. All 7 recipe files (door, wall, player, monster-ice-daemon, item-cred-chip, npc-ripperdoc, plus floor.ts itself) and src/atlas/generate.ts and tests/atlas/registry.test.ts now import RecipeContext from ./types or ./recipes/types. The single remaining import via the floor.ts re-export shim is in src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts:17, intentionally untouched because that file is in RULES_FILES — modifying it would bump rulesetTextHash → rulesetVersion → require a cross-OS matrix re-run; addressed when the registry naturally needs to be touched (Phase 6+ when item / NPC content extends the registry).
phase_4_a_2_nit_N6_recipe_context_reloca.scope_note: Code-reviewer flagged the registry import as a partial-migration nit; non-blocking because the type is single-defined (no DRY violation in the source-of-truth sense).

phase_4_a_2_nit_N7_fflate_version_pin.evidence: tests/build/fflate-version-pin.test.ts: a single test that reads package.json directly, parses it, and asserts devDependencies.fflate === '0.8.2'. This is the explicit complementary trip-wire to the implicit golden-hash check in atlas-encoder-cross-runtime; a `package-lock.json` integrity bump or a careless caret/tilde introduction fails this test loudly without waiting for the encoder golden mismatch. Bumping the pin is a `rulesetVersion` bump (encoder bytes change → atlasBinaryHash bumps) and per addendum N1 + N2 requires architecture-red-team review + cross-OS matrix re-verification.

phase_3_a_2_carry_forwards_deferred.evidence: Phase 3.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards (applyFloorEntry redundant param at run.ts, dirOrdinalForStep export-only-by-tests at ai.ts, ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES shared constant at combat.ts, tick unknown-action defensive type-check at turn.ts, e2e SIM_DIGEST duplication note at tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts) are intentionally NOT addressed in 5.A.1. Per docs/PHASES.md Phase 5.A.1 acceptance criterion, these are addressed 'where they touch files Phase 5.A.2 will modify'. Phase 5.A.2's scope is src/render/, src/input/, src/ui/, plus orchestration changes in src/main.ts; it does NOT modify src/sim/{run,ai,combat,turn}.ts (it consumes the existing harness and tick read-only). The tick unknown-action defensive type-check is also subject to the Phase 1 'additive vocabulary' frozen contract — adding a throw on unknown types would technically be a contract bump. Defer to a Phase 6+ phase that naturally needs to extend the action vocabulary (e.g., inventory pickup actions in Phase 6).

architecture_md_phase_5_section.evidence: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md gains a 'Phase 5 frozen contracts (renderer + input + ui)' section between Phase 4 contracts and Build-time constants (~100 new lines + 2 new rows in Lint rule inventory). Coverage: renderer is a read-only sink on sim state (no mutation, no PRNG consumption, no tick() calls; runtime architectural test in tests/render/render-readonly.test.ts called out as 5.A.2 deliverable); renderer cannot import core/streams.ts or sim/combat.ts (lint rule body lands in 5.A.2's eslint.config.js extension); atlas loader called once at run start with the addendum-N7 placeholder-refusal + ATLAS_MISSING + hash-mismatch error messages already implemented in src/atlas/loader.ts; input descriptor maps to Phase 1 Action schema (keypress → Action is configuration, no rulesetVersion bump); HUD is a read-only sink on RunState; diagnostic surface preserved (Phase 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B Playwright assertions keep passing); three new layer-table entries (src/render/, src/input/, src/ui/) with imports-allowed and imports-forbidden lists each (no float, no time except input-event timestamps, no async except atlas-loader startup); single-orchestrator rule (only src/main.ts wires input → sim → render → ui); deferred Phase 5 contracts (harness mapgen exception migration to src/run/, frame-rate budget deferred to Phase 9, accessibility deferred to Phase 9). Lint rule inventory adds two new rows for the render/input/ui boundaries with explicit 'rule body lands in 5.A.2' notes.

no_net_new_render_input_ui_code.evidence: git status --short shows zero new files under src/render/, src/input/, or src/ui/. ls /workspace/src/ confirms those directories don't exist yet. The two new working-tree files are src/atlas/recipes/types.ts (Phase 4.A.2 carry-forward N6) and tests/build/fflate-version-pin.test.ts (Phase 4.A.2 carry-forward N7) — both are scope-correct atlas-pipeline drift items, not Phase 5.A.2 surface.

code_review.evidence: artifacts/code-review-phase-5-A-1.md: verdict APPROVE. 0 blockers. All four PHASES.md acceptance criteria pass. Three cosmetic findings: (a) N6 partial-migration on src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts:17 + tests/atlas/registry.test.ts:12 — the test-case import migrated in this same approval pass; the registry import is intentionally untouched because it is in RULES_FILES (would bump rulesetVersion); (b) lint rule inventory in ARCHITECTURE.md was missing rows for the new render/input/ui boundaries — two new rows added in this same approval pass with 'rule body lands in 5.A.2' deferred-implementation notes; (c) tsconfig.tsbuildinfo not gitignored — pre-existing, out of Phase 5.A.1 scope. Final gates verified post-fixup: 824 passing, lint clean, typecheck clean, build byte-identical.

follow_ups_carried_forward_to_phase_5_a_: src/render/canvas.ts (tile renderer; reads RunState read-only; canvas drawImage from the loaded atlas; no PRNG consumption; no float arithmetic; deterministic frame loop driven by sim ticks rather than rAF).

src/input/keyboard.ts (keypress event handler producing Action descriptors per the Phase 1 schema; arrow keys / WASD → move dir; space → wait; single key → attack dir; > → descend; pinned keybindings as configuration, not schema).

src/ui/hud.ts (HP / eddies / floor indicator / fingerprint widget; reads RunState.player + RunState.outcome + RunState.fingerprintInputs read-only; recomputes fingerprint each frame from deterministic inputs).

src/main.ts atlas-loader integration — call loadAtlas() (already implemented in src/atlas/loader.ts) at run start; refuse on PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION or hash mismatch with the addendum-N7-pinned messages; the Phase 5.A.2 commit is the first caller of loadAtlas (defined-but-not-called pattern continues until then).

src/main.ts diagnostic→playable transition — preserve the existing diagnostic page sections (self-test, build-info, floor-preview, scripted-playthrough, atlas-preview) so the cross-runtime Playwright suite from Phases 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B keeps passing alongside the new playable-game UI; sections may be moved into a collapsible <details> element but DOM ids and window.__*__ flags are preserved.

Phase 5 layer-import lint rule bodies in eslint.config.js (the rows are documented in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md's lint rule inventory; the rule bodies themselves are 5.A.2 deliverables): no-restricted-imports for src/render/** banning src/core/streams.ts, src/sim/combat.ts, src/sim/turn.ts, src/sim/run.ts; no-restricted-imports for src/input/** banning the same sim write paths; no-restricted-imports for src/ui/** with the same scope. Fixture tests in eslint-rules/__tests__/ to confirm.
Architectural test tests/render/render-readonly.test.ts — calls the renderer with a deeply-frozen RunState and confirms no mutation throws (5.A.2).
follow_ups_non_blocking_cosmetic: src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts:17 still imports RecipeContext via the floor.ts re-export shim (instead of directly from ./recipes/types). Intentionally deferred: the registry file is in RULES_FILES, so editing it bumps rulesetTextHash → rulesetVersion → atlasBinaryHash → cross-OS matrix would need to re-verify. Address when the registry naturally needs touching (Phase 6+ when item or NPC content extends the registry).
tsconfig.tsbuildinfo is not gitignored. Pre-existing; out of Phase 5.A.1 scope. Address as a small chore at any phase boundary.

Phase 3.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards still held back from 5.A.1 per the established deferral rule: applyFloorEntry redundant param, dirOrdinalForStep export comment, ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES shared constant, tick unknown-action defensive type-check, e2e SIM_DIGEST duplication. Address when the corresponding files are naturally touched (likely Phase 6+ when action vocabulary extends).
next_phase: Phase 5.A.2 — sandbox-verifiable renderer + input + ui implementation per docs/PHASES.md. Acceptance criteria summary: a human can play a real run end-to-end on the deployed URL; renderer reads from sim state and never writes to it (architectural test); src/render/** disallowed from importing src/core/streams.ts or src/sim/combat.ts (lint rule); atlas hash mismatch produces a clear error and aborts the run (already implemented in src/atlas/loader.ts; 5.A.2 wires the loader into src/main.ts startup); the diagnostic page's existing sections continue to render so the cross-runtime Playwright suite from Phases 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B keeps passing alongside the new playable-game UI; npm ci && npm run lint && npm run test && npm run build && npm run test:e2e all green inside the sandbox. Lead agent: frontend-builder (UI, render, input). Reviewer: code-reviewer (architecture compliance), qa-playwright (user-visible browser flow).
